name: MCP Server Email
what_it_does: MCP email server enabling AI agents to send and receive emails via SMTP/IMAP — composing and sending emails, reading inbox messages, searching email history, managing folders, and integrating email communication into agent-driven notification, outreach, and communication workflow automation.
use_cases: Sending automated email notifications from workflow agents
Reading and processing incoming emails from inbox management agents
Composing and sending email replies from customer service agents
Monitoring email for triggers and events from automation agents
Sending reports and digests via email from reporting agents
Managing email workflows and folder organization from productivity agents
not_for: Mass email marketing (use dedicated email marketing platforms to avoid spam classification)
Transactional email at scale (use SendGrid, Mailgun, or AWS SES instead)
Teams needing email tracking, analytics, or deliverability optimization
best_when: An agent needs to send or read emails as part of a workflow — for notifications, replies, or monitoring without requiring a full email marketing platform.
avoid_when: You need high-volume email, deliverability guarantees, or marketing features — use dedicated email service providers instead.

auth.notes: SMTP/IMAP credentials required: email address, password (or App Password for Gmail/Outlook), server host and port. Gmail requires App Password with 2FA enabled.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for personal email accounts; email provider sending limits apply
pricing.paid_tiers: Email provider plans for higher volume
pricing.notes: MCP server is free and open source. Email provider limits apply (Gmail: 500/day free, 2000/day Google Workspace).

performance.rate_limits: Email provider dependent: Gmail 500 emails/day free, SMTP server limits vary

known_agent_gotchas: Gmail requires App Password (not regular password) with 2FA enabled — common setup confusion
IMAP must be enabled in email account settings — not enabled by default on all providers
Email credentials stored in config — use environment variables, never hardcode passwords
Community MCP with minimal security hardening — review code before use with real credentials
Attachment handling may be limited — check supported formats before relying on attachment features
Sending rate limits vary by provider — implement delays to avoid spam classification
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS/STARTTLS enforced for SMTP/IMAP. Store credentials as environment variables. Use App Passwords for Gmail/Outlook. Email content may be sensitive — ensure secure credential handling. Community MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Use App Passwords. Environment variables for credentials. Community MCP — review code.
